subject: [PATCH v3 0/2] mm/vmpressure: reduce CPU, memory and code overhead on cgroup v2

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a missing Acked-by tag in the patch and apologized for the oversight.
reviews.raw_body:
Shakeel had acked the previous version, but I forgot to carry it over,
sorry about that!

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary: Reviewer suggested a simpler condition to skip v2 + tree=true case in vmpressure() and acknowledged the patch, but expressed no strong feelings about it.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged a mistake in their previous patch submission, specifically forgetting to move code from vmpressure-v1.c to memcontrol-v1.c and not including Shakeel's ack on v2. They asked reviewer Andrew to apply a fixlet or for them to respin the patch.

Author addressed a concern about the complexity of the guard in vmpressure() by simplifying it, replacing two conditions with a single boolean comparison between cgroup_subsys_on_dfl() and tree.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah sorry about this! I just amended my last patch to move code
from vmpressure-v1.c to memcontrol-v1.c and just sent it, without
other changes. Forgot Shakeels ack on v2 as well :(

Andrew would you mind applying the below fixlet? I can also respin
if its easier. Thanks!!

---

From: Usama Arif <usama.arif@linux.dev>
Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 09:25:05 -0700
Subject: [fixlet] mm/vmpressure: skip tree=true accounting on cgroup v2

Simplify the guard. Both cgroup_subsys_on_dfl() and tree are bool, so
the two combinations that have no consumer (v1 + tree=false, v2 +
tree=true) are exactly the cases where dfl == tree.

Suggested-by: Johannes Weiner <hannes@cmpxchg.org>
Signed-off-by: Usama Arif <usama.arif@linux.dev>
---
 mm/vmpressure.c | 3 +--
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/mm/vmpressure.c b/mm/vmpressure.c
index 14470141bbe6..9629240d77ad 100644
--- a/mm/vmpressure.c
+++ b/mm/vmpressure.c
@@ -120,8 +120,7 @@ void vmpressure(gfp_t gfp, int order, struct mem_cgroup *memcg, bool tree,
 	 *   cgroup v1 + tree=true  -> userspace eventfds (memory.pressure_level)
 	 * Skip the other two: nothing consumes the result.
 	 */
-	if ((!cgroup_subsys_on_dfl(memory_cgrp_subsys) && !tree) ||
-	    (cgroup_subsys_on_dfl(memory_cgrp_subsys) && tree))
+	if (cgroup_subsys_on_dfl(memory_cgrp_subsys) == tree)
 		return;
 
 	vmpr = memcg_to_vmpressure(memcg);
-- 
2.53.0-Meta

2026-06-30.patch_summary: This patch reduces CPU, memory, and code overhead on cgroup v2 by eliminating unnecessary work in the vmpressure subsystem. It does this by extending an existing early return to skip a contended path where reclaimers can serialize on a single global lock when tree=true. Additionally, it splits the v1 userspace eventfd interface into a separate file (mm/memcontrol-v1.c) built only when CONFIG_MEMCG_V1 is enabled, reducing code size and complexity. This patch is a step towards eventually making vmpressure CONFIG_MEMCG_V1 only.
